title: Skill infra overhaul: contracts, run-tasks safety, pipeline gate
summary: Consolidates three related grill sessions into one ready seed for a single task breakdown. Workstream A (contracts): make contracts/ the single source of truth and define one shared, walkable provenance concept (producer + source) reused by every lineage contract via cross-file $ref, replacing task's required prd. Workstream B (run-tasks-safety): stop the run-tasks test gate from pinning all cores by running affected tests only with a worker cap, a hard timeout, serialized runners, execution hygiene, and an explicit status enum. Workstream C (pipeline-gate): a judgment/hands/AFK trichotomy with a hard state-based gate that forces every open_thread to resolve/defer/reject before a seed graduates, a deferred bucket and structured out_of_scope, a disposable seed lifecycle with ADRs as the durable record, a grill-with-docs reconciliation pass, and removal of the to-tasks scope quiz and save-location gate. Two decisions were resolved during consolidation: to-prd-html is a producer, and the kind/ref correlation is enforced in-schema via if/then. open_threads is empty, so this seed is ready.
decisions: [contracts] contracts/ is the single source of truth. Every skill-local schema artifact is demoted to either a pure worked instance that must validate clean against its contract, or a pointer stub that redirects to the contract. No skill-local file may redefine shape, required fields, or field descriptions.

[contracts] Delete skills/to-seed/resources/seed-example.json. Move one worked, internally coherent example into seed-schema.json's native top-level 'examples' keyword. One file holds shape, descriptions, and example, so there is no cross-file copy to drift.

[contracts] Provenance is captured by two fields, not one: 'producer' (which skill wrote the file) and 'source' (what the file was derived from). Splitting them keeps each axis clean and lets the chain be reverse-traced.

[contracts] Provenance is defined ONCE in a new contracts/provenance-schema.json holding $defs.producer and $defs.source. seed-schema and task-schema reference it via cross-file $ref (e.g. {"$ref": "provenance-schema#/$defs/source"}). This is the only physical mechanism that honors 'define once'; embedded copies or build-time inlining were rejected.

[contracts] 'producer' is a closed enum seeded with every lineage-producing skill plus 'manual' (the escape hatch for hand-authored files). 'source.kind' is a closed enum ['seed','prd','session'] with 'task' deferred until a real producer derives from a task file.

[contracts] Both provenance fields are enforced enums (not open strings) for consistency and typo protection. The maintenance cost is absorbed by codifying enum upkeep as a step in register-skill: a new lineage producer appends its name to the producer enum.

[contracts] 'source' is a nullable object {kind, ref}. source:null means the file is the root of its lineage chain. ref is a path/URL string or null. A reverse-trace tool switches on source.kind to know how to follow source.ref upward.

[contracts] Retire the seed 'base_seed' field. Mode-2 (handoff-merge) seeds now express lineage through source = {kind:'seed', ref:'<parent seed path>'}. One lineage mechanism for the whole pipeline instead of two.

[contracts] Reconcile the seed naming collision: seed-schema's current 'source' field (a producer string like 'to-seed') is renamed to 'producer', and the new 'source' object is added. seed and task now share identical provenance vocabulary.
[contracts] task-schema drops the required 'prd' field and adds required 'producer' + 'source'. PRD becomes just one possible source.kind among several, not an enforced default.

[contracts] task-schema also gets exactly one embedded coherence example (a to-tasks-from-seed file with a HITL+AFK mix, real blocked_by chains, one browser_verify, one follow_up whose trigger_task references a real task id). Not one-per-producer: task file structure does not vary by producer, only the provenance fields do.
[contracts] validate-schema.sh gets a one-time upgrade: load every contracts/*.json into a referencing.Registry (Python jsonschema) so cross-file $ref by bare $id resolves for all 11 calling skills.

[contracts] validate-schema.sh also gains a meta-check that extracts examples[*] from a schema and validates each against that same schema, so the in-file coherence examples cannot drift from their own properties.
[contracts] validate-schema.sh emits a clear schema_version-mismatch error pointing the user to regenerate via /to-seed, instead of a cryptic property error.

[contracts] Hard cutover, no migration tooling. schema_version is a const: seed bumps 2 -> 3, task bumps 1 -> 2. Old on-disk files fail validation by design; clean-scaffolding wipes disposable docs and nothing is in flight.

[contracts] Consumer to-e2e-tasks repoints from the old task 'prd' field to 'source.ref': when ref is non-null (kind seed|prd) it loads that artifact for context; when kind is 'session' (ref null) it falls through to the existing prompt-or-skip path.

[contracts] Consumer tasks-to-linear derives the Linear title prefix from the task file's own filename slug, not from prd. The task file always has a slug; source.ref can be null. The prefix identifies the task batch, not an upstream document.
[contracts] Delete the stray resources/task-schema.md and repoint to-tasks (line 75) at contracts/task-schema.json. It violates the source-of-truth rule and is a second task schema doc.

[contracts] RESOLVED (was open): to-prd-html IS a lineage producer. Add it to the producer enum; the HTML PRD stamps producer:'to-prd-html' and source:{kind:'seed', ref:<seed path>}. This keeps the chain walkable: when to-tasks runs off an HTML PRD it sets source:{kind:'prd', ref:<html path>}, so the HTML must carry its own source or the prd->seed link breaks at that node.

[contracts] RESOLVED (was open): enforce the provenance kind/ref correlation in-schema via JSON Schema if/then in provenance-schema.json $defs.source: kind='session' requires ref=null; kind in ['seed','prd'] requires a non-null string ref. validate-schema.sh rejects mismatched stamps. Chosen over prose convention to stay consistent with the enforced-enum thesis; a 2-branch correlation is not gold-plating.

[run-tasks-safety] Local run-tasks gate runs affected tests only; CI (CircleCI yarn test --maxWorkers=2, full suite) remains the full-suite regression backstop. A full-suite precommit hook is optional/later.

[run-tasks-safety] Affected selection uses an explicit list: vitest related <touched-source-files> plus touched test files run directly. Not git --changed (avoids the untracked-new-test-file hole mid-TDD).
[run-tasks-safety] When 0 affected tests are selected, the gate WARN-passes (never silent, never blocks). No file-type tiering between source and docs/config changes.
[run-tasks-safety] Always-on worker cap of --maxWorkers=75% on every gate test run (leaves headroom on an 8-core box, no full lockup).

[run-tasks-safety] detect_tooling.py owns a framework-aware test_affected template with a {files} placeholder and the worker cap baked in. run-tasks substitutes the touched files; test-runner runs the command verbatim and stays a dumb executor.

[run-tasks-safety] Cross-framework: vitest uses related (two-segment: direct test files + related source dependents); jest uses --findRelatedTests; pytest has no native affected selection so detect_tooling.py emits a null template and run-tasks falls back to a capped full suite plus WARN. No pytest-testmon investment now.
[run-tasks-safety] Hard 180s wall-clock timeout on the test run. On timeout, kill the whole process group (reap vitest workers, not just the parent) and treat it as a gate failure.

[run-tasks-safety] Serialize test-runners across workspaces (one vitest process at a time). Lint-runners stay parallel. Parallelism lives inside vitest's bounded worker pool, never across per-file or per-workspace agents.

[run-tasks-safety] Execution hygiene in test-runner: run the command in the foreground and block on it (no background, no ps-grep busy-wait), use the resolved local binary not npx, and fail fast if deps are missing rather than auto-installing.

[run-tasks-safety] runner-result-contract carries an explicit status enum {pass|fail|warn|timeout|deps-missing} alongside counts. run-tasks branches on status: pass/warn proceed (warn prints a coverage-deferred message), fail/timeout/deps-missing block.

[pipeline-gate] Adopt a trichotomy for classifying work: needs human JUDGMENT (a decision that changes what gets built) resolves in the grill and never becomes a task; needs human HANDS (a keyboard action the AI cannot do AFK, e.g. enable a flag, add a credential, configure DNS, seed data) is the ONLY legitimate task-level HITL; needs neither is AFK.

[pipeline-gate] Keep the HITL label at the task level but narrow its meaning to hands-only. Remove 'architectural decision / design review' from to-tasks SKILL.md line 40 so the skill no longer sanctions decision-tasks.

[pipeline-gate] Install a hard, durable, state-based gate. Every open_thread must exit via exactly one of three doors: RESOLVE (into decisions), DEFER (into a new deferred[] field), or REJECT (into out_of_scope). Nothing stays undecided.
[pipeline-gate] Seed status is DERIVED, never hand-set: status is 'ready' iff open_threads == [], otherwise 'draft'.
[pipeline-gate] to-seed refuses to finish a draft seed and loops the user back through handoff -> grill until every judgment thread is closed and status flips to ready.
[pipeline-gate] to-tasks HARD-REFUSES a draft seed. This durable state-based check is the airtight backstop and survives context compaction, which a transient prompt did not.

[pipeline-gate] Add a new deferred[] field whose items are { text, rationale, context, source_seed }. No revisit field and no dependency field: source_seed is the loose dependency hook, and real dependency modeling is left to the future consumer. deferred is record-only for now.
[pipeline-gate] Restructure out_of_scope items to { text, rationale } so they read as negative context that to-tasks can consume.

[pipeline-gate] open_threads holds genuine unresolved JUDGMENT only. Decided-but-conditional implementation details (e.g. 'use Registry API if jsonschema >=4.18 else RefResolver') are NOT open threads; they move into a task's acceptance criteria.

[pipeline-gate] A ready seed is a fork point with two consumers: to-tasks reads decisions + spec for now-work and reads out_of_scope as a negative guard so it never scopes or tracers a rejected thing; a future ticket-gen skill reads deferred for later-work.

[pipeline-gate] Provenance lives entirely in the disposable scaffolding layer (seeds, PRDs, task files stamp each other). Code and ADRs are the permanent record. Seeds are therefore deleted once the ENTIRE dependency chain is consumed.

[pipeline-gate] clean-scaffolding becomes lineage-aware: it deletes a chain ATOMICALLY and only when the whole subtree is consumed (never partial, which would dangle refs), and it REFUSES to delete a chain whose architecture decisions are not yet captured in an ADR.
[pipeline-gate] grill-me stays write-free: it interviews and produces a seed only, no doc writes.

[pipeline-gate] grill-with-docs remains the sole ADR promoter and keeps its sparse three-criteria filter (hard to reverse + surprising without context + a real trade-off), which intentionally captures architecture-only decisions and lets mechanical decisions live in the diff.
[pipeline-gate] No seed-to-ADR extraction step. Extracting from a compressed seed after the fact is lossier and context-free.

[pipeline-gate] grill-with-docs gains a SESSION-END reconciliation pass: while the conversation is still live and decisions are final, it diffs the final decision set against the inline-written ADRs, supersedes stale ones, and captures architectural decisions reached late.

[pipeline-gate] to-tasks removes the scope/granularity quiz entirely. On a ready seed it slices, classifies HITL/AFK, infers follow-ups, and writes autonomously. Its only remaining interactive pause is the draft-seed refusal.
[pipeline-gate] to-tasks removes the save-location confirmation gate and writes straight to the derived docs/tasks/ path, ending with a single final output line: 'docs created here: <path>'.
[pipeline-gate] Keep the HITL enum value rather than renaming it to MANUAL or INTERVENTION; only its definition changes.
[pipeline-gate] Leave branching untouched this round. The branching prompt and shared branching-strategy.md are deferred to a dedicated future effort.
next_action: Run /to-tasks on this consolidated seed to generate one task breakdown across the [contracts], [run-tasks-safety], and [pipeline-gate] workstreams, sequencing edits to shared files (to-tasks, to-seed, run-tasks, seed-schema, task-schema) so they do not collide, on the in-flight feat/contract-update branch.
problem_statement: Three tangentially related improvements to the skill tree were grilled in separate sessions and produced three separate seeds that touch overlapping files. Generating tasks from them independently would collide on shared edits (to-tasks, to-seed, run-tasks, seed-schema, task-schema) and could not be sequenced. One of the seeds also still carried unresolved judgment, which under the new pipeline rules blocks task generation.
solution: Consolidate the three seeds into one ready seed with decisions tagged by workstream, resolve the two outstanding judgment calls (to-prd-html producer status; kind/ref enforcement), reclassify the one decided-but-conditional thread into task acceptance criteria, and hand the single seed to /to-tasks so the overlapping edits are planned and sequenced together.
evidence: Three seeds on disk created 2026-06-03 (16:10, 16:49, 18:31). Cross-seed analysis found 7+ shared file targets, one auto-resolved conflict (the jsonschema-version conditional), and 2 genuine open decisions in the contracts seed which are the same T-0001/T-0002 that caused the run-tasks decision-pause that motivated the pipeline-gate work.
implementation_decisions: [contracts] New file contracts/provenance-schema.json with $id 'provenance-schema', exposing $defs.producer (closed enum string, now including to-prd-html) and $defs.source (oneOf: {kind enum [seed,prd,session], ref string|null} or null) WITH an if/then enforcing the kind/ref correlation (session->ref null; seed|prd->ref non-null).

[contracts] seed-schema.json (v3): rename 'source' -> 'producer' via $ref; add 'source' via $ref; remove base_seed; add top-level 'examples' with one coherent seed instance; keep additionalProperties:false.
[contracts] task-schema.json (v2): remove 'prd' from properties and required; add required 'producer' and 'source' via $ref; add top-level 'examples' with one coherent task-file instance.

[contracts] validate-schema.sh: build a referencing.Registry from all contracts/*.json keyed by bare $id; add examples[*] self-validation meta-check; add explicit schema_version-mismatch messaging. Acceptance criteria include the jsonschema-version conditional: detect the installed version and use the referencing.Registry API on >=4.18, the deprecated RefResolver below it (design unchanged, resolver impl differs).
[contracts] register-skill/SKILL.md: add a codified step requiring a new lineage producer to append itself to the provenance-schema producer enum.

[contracts] Producers to stamp producer/source: to-tasks, debug, code-review, improve-component, to-e2e-tasks, prototype, to-seed, AND to-prd-html. Consumers to update: to-e2e-tasks (read source.ref), tasks-to-linear (slug from task filename).
[contracts] Delete skills/to-seed/resources/seed-example.json and resources/task-schema.md; repoint to-tasks line 75 at contracts/task-schema.json.

[run-tasks-safety] detect_tooling.py: add per-framework test_affected template resolution (vitest/jest real templates, pytest null), with --maxWorkers=75% baked into the template and a {files} placeholder.

[run-tasks-safety] agents/test-runner.md: accept a touched-files input and substitute {files}; run foreground/blocking; use resolved local binary not npx; fail fast on missing deps; wrap the run in a portable 180s timeout (gtimeout or portable fallback, since macOS has no native timeout) that kills the process group; emit explicit status.

[run-tasks-safety] skills/run-tasks/SKILL.md: pass the per-workspace touched-file list to the test-runner; serialize test-runners across workspaces while keeping lint-runners parallel; branch the gate on the status enum (warn proceeds with a message, fail/timeout/deps-missing block).
[run-tasks-safety] contracts/runner-result-contract.md and runner-result-schema.json: add a status enum {pass|fail|warn|timeout|deps-missing} alongside the existing {passed,failed,skipped} counts.

[pipeline-gate] contracts/seed-schema.json: add deferred[] ({text, rationale, context, source_seed}); restructure out_of_scope items to {text, rationale}; add a derived status enum (ready|draft); tighten the open_threads definition to judgment-only. Fold into the same v3 seed-schema change above; do not fork.

[pipeline-gate] to-seed/SKILL.md: route each open_thread into one of decisions|deferred|out_of_scope; derive and write status; hard-loop a draft seed back through handoff -> grill instead of the current non-blocking menu offer; stop mis-filing decided-but-conditional details as open_threads. Also repoint synthesis at contracts/seed-schema.json and change Mode-2 merge to set source={kind:'seed', ref:<parent>} instead of base_seed (shared with [contracts]).

[pipeline-gate] to-tasks/SKILL.md: redefine HITL to hands-only and remove 'architectural decision / design review' at line 40; hard-refuse a draft seed; delete the scope/granularity quiz (lines 90-111); delete the save-location gate (lines 121-130); read out_of_scope as a negative guard; emit a final 'docs created here: <path>' line. (Coordinate with the [contracts] line-75 repoint in the same file.)

[pipeline-gate] run-tasks/SKILL.md: HITL is now hands-only by construction, so the existing pause logic stays but decision-HITL can no longer occur. (Coordinate with the [run-tasks-safety] gate changes in the same file.)

[pipeline-gate] grill-with-docs/SKILL.md: add a session-end reconciliation pass that diffs the final decision set against inline-written ADRs, supersedes stale ones, and captures late-reached architectural decisions.

[pipeline-gate] clean-scaffolding: implement lineage-aware atomic chain deletion (never partial) plus an ADR backstop that refuses to delete a chain whose architecture decisions are not yet in an ADR.
[pipeline-gate] Sibling emitters to-e2e-tasks, debug, and improve-component: inherit the hands-only HITL definition, and the draft-seed refusal where they consume seeds.
testing_decisions: No unit tests for these claude-code-shared skill/schema/agent/script changes, per the standing infra convention.

Validate schema changes with validate-schema.sh, including the new examples[*] self-validation and the kind/ref if/then. Dry-run the grill -> seed -> to-tasks -> run-tasks flow manually to confirm the gate, the removed prompts, and the test-gate worker cap/timeout behave as intended.
The runner status enum is validated against contracts/runner-result-contract.md.

out_of_scope.rationale: It is an ephemeral verdict, not a persisted lineage document.

out_of_scope.text: [contracts] Migration script or dual-version validator acceptance
out_of_scope.rationale: Hard cutover only; old on-disk files fail by design.
Deferred until a real producer derives from a task file.
No investment in pytest testmon at this stage.
Deferred; optional for a later effort.

out_of_scope.text: [run-tasks-safety] Per-N dividing of the worker cap across parallel workspace runners
out_of_scope.rationale: Rejected in favor of serializing runners.
Deferred to its own dedicated grill.

out_of_scope.text: [pipeline-gate] Building the future ticket-gen skill that consumes deferred[]
out_of_scope.rationale: The data contract is instrumented now; the skill is built later.
Explicitly rejected in favor of live reconciliation in grill-with-docs.

out_of_scope.text: [pipeline-gate] Migrating existing on-disk task files to the new hands-only HITL semantics
out_of_scope.rationale: Not needed; these are disposable infra artifacts.

risks_and_tradeoffs: The three workstreams edit overlapping files (to-tasks/SKILL.md, to-seed/SKILL.md, run-tasks/SKILL.md, seed-schema.json, task-schema.json). Task generation MUST sequence edits to each shared file so the [contracts], [run-tasks-safety], and [pipeline-gate] changes do not clobber one another. This is the primary reason the seeds were consolidated.

[contracts] Hard cutover with no migration means any on-disk seed or task file from the old schema versions fails validation immediately; this is by design and relies on clean-scaffolding plus nothing being in flight.
[run-tasks-safety] The local gate runs affected-only and leans on CI as the full-suite regression backstop; a gap in 'affected' selection could let a regression through locally until CI.

[pipeline-gate] The hard gate relocates friction upstream into grill/seed rather than removing it; a write-free grill-me on architectural work writes no ADRs, so only the clean-scaffolding ADR backstop prevents losing rationale on cleanup.
[pipeline-gate] Deleting seeds after chain consumption makes provenance refs transient by design; correctness depends on atomic, never-partial chain deletion.
[pipeline-gate] The branching prompt remains a second interactive pause in to-tasks, so to-tasks is not fully prompt-free after this work.
further_notes: This seed consolidates three source seeds, which it supersedes: docs/seeds/20260603-1610-contracts-provenance-source-of-truth.json, docs/seeds/20260603-1649-run-tasks-cpu-blowup-prevention.json, docs/seeds/20260603-1831-brainstorm-to-tasks-pipeline-redesign.json.
Decisions, implementation_decisions, and out_of_scope are tagged [contracts] / [run-tasks-safety] / [pipeline-gate] to keep the three workstreams legible and give to-tasks natural slice seams.

Two decisions were newly resolved during consolidation (to-prd-html is a producer; kind/ref enforced via if/then), closing the contracts seed's open threads. Its jsonschema-version thread was reclassified into the validate-schema.sh task's acceptance criteria per the pipeline-gate rule that decided-but-conditional details are not open threads.
base_seed is single-valued (and being retired in this very work), so the three source seeds are recorded here in further_notes rather than via base_seed.
